item.title: NLP Series [Part 2] · Word2Vec-SkipGram-Negative-Sampling
item.description: Most of the steps are similar to our notebook on Word2Vec without negative sampling. The key changes happen with the dataset where we provide a list of indices for negative sample for each forward pass as well as the loss function. In this notebook, we will train a Word2Vec skip-gram model with negative sampling from scratch on some text and inspect the trained embeddings at the end. The first step for using any kind of NLP pipeline is to vectorize the text.

item.title: NLP Series [Part 1] · Word2Vec-SkipGram
item.description: In this notebook, we will train a Word2Vec skip-gram model from scratch on some text and inspect the trained embeddings at the end. The first step for using any kind of NLP pipeline is to vectorize the text. Traditionally, we used to do this using one-hot representation of vectors. This had various downsides like: Vectors tend to be very long as their size depends on the vocabulary size of the corpus(which grows with the corpus size).
